summary: Dependency-confusion canary exfiltrates CI/host identifiers on postinstall
details: Package @luminarycloudinternal/lcvis-st@9999.0.1 is a dependency-confusion placeholder targeting the internal-looking @luminarycloudinternal npm scope with a version (9999.0.1) chosen to win resolution over an internal-registry package of the same name. Its postinstall.js runs on `npm install` and issues an HTTPS GET to poc-luminary-npm-1782987043.testingboxes.com carrying installer-side identifiers gathered from os.hostname(), the npm user-agent, and GitHub Actions environment variables (GITHUB_REPOSITORY, GITHUB_SHA, GITHUB_REF, GITHUB_WORKFLOW, RUNNER_NAME). Any organization build that misconfigures scope registry routing will silently transmit hostname and private-repo/workflow identifiers to the third-party endpoint. Although the README self-identifies the package as a Bugcrowd research canary, the mechanism (scope squat + version overshoot + auto-executing install-time beacon to an author-controlled host) is a functioning dependency-confusion attack against builds of the targeted organization.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
